Methods

Results

conflicts.title: Chandelier cell effect on pyramidal neurons: excitatory vs inhibitory
conflicts.description: Chandelier cells target the axon initial segment of pyramidal neurons, where GABA reversal potential may be depolarized relative to rest. Whether this depolarization is excitatory (facilitates firing) or inhibitory (shunting inhibition prevents firing) has been debated for over 20 years.

side_a.position: Chandelier cells are excitatory at the AIS due to depolarized GABA reversal potential

side_b.position: Chandelier cells are inhibitory via shunting inhibition despite depolarized GABA reversal potential

conflicts.notes: Emerging consensus is that the effect is context-dependent: chandelier cells can be depolarizing but still inhibitory via shunting, or excitatory depending on membrane potential state.

conflicts.title: Cardinal classes sufficient vs insufficient for cortical interneuron taxonomy
conflicts.description: Whether the four cardinal classes (PV, SST, VIP, Lamp5/Id2) capture all cortical interneuron diversity, or whether significant populations fall outside these categories.

side_a.position: Cardinal classes are largely sufficient; most interneurons can be assigned to one of these classes

side_b.position: Significant interneuron populations (5-10%) cannot be cleanly assigned to cardinal classes; additional categories needed

conflicts.notes: Transcriptomic data increasingly support cardinal classes as hierarchically organized with extensive subtype diversity within each class, but some cells (e.g., CCK basket cells) are problematic to assign.

conflicts.title: CCK vs PV basket cells: functionally distinct vs redundant perisomatic inhibitors
conflicts.description: Both CCK+ and PV+ basket cells provide perisomatic inhibition to pyramidal neurons. Whether they serve fundamentally different functions or provide redundant inhibitory control.

side_a.position: CCK and PV basket cells serve distinct, complementary functions - PV for clockwork precision, CCK for mood/state modulation

side_b.position: Functional overlap between CCK and PV basket cells is more substantial than typically assumed

conflicts.notes: Most evidence supports functional distinction: PV cells have fast, reliable, synchrony-promoting inhibition while CCK cells have slower, mood-modulated inhibition. But in vivo data remain scarce for CCK cells due to lack of clean genetic access.

conflicts.title: NDNF/neurogliaform cell volume transmission: predominant vs minor mode of signaling
conflicts.description: Whether neurogliaform cells primarily operate through non-synaptic (volume) GABA transmission affecting GABA-B receptors, or whether this is a supplementary mode alongside conventional synaptic transmission.

side_a.position: Volume transmission is the defining feature and primary signaling mode of neurogliaform cells

side_b.position: Neurogliaform cells also make conventional synapses; volume transmission supplements rather than replaces synaptic signaling

conflicts.notes: Evidence indicates neurogliaform cells engage both synaptic (GABA-A) and volume (GABA-B) transmission, with the ratio possibly depending on the activity level. Volume transmission appears to be particularly prominent compared to other interneuron types.

conflicts.title: Lamp5 as a coherent transcriptomic class vs lumping of diverse cell types
conflicts.description: Whether Lamp5 marks a functionally coherent interneuron class or groups together diverse cell types (neurogliaform, single bouquet, etc.) that share gene expression but differ functionally.

side_a.position: Lamp5 defines a coherent class with shared developmental origin and gene expression programs

side_b.position: Lamp5 class contains functionally heterogeneous subtypes that differ more than members of other cardinal classes

conflicts.notes: Transcriptomic data place neurogliaform cells, single bouquet cells, and other subtypes within the Lamp5 class, but functional differences among these subtypes may be substantial.

evidence_gaps.description: Limited in vivo functional data on CCK basket cells due to lack of clean Cre driver lines
Chandelier cell excitatory vs inhibitory debate lacks definitive in vivo measurements of GABA reversal potential at the AIS in behaving animals
Fraction of interneurons that cannot be cleanly assigned to cardinal classes is poorly quantified across brain regions
Cross-species comparison of NDNF/Lamp5 subtypes is limited; most data from mouse with some human Patch-seq
Volume transmission from neurogliaform cells is primarily demonstrated in slice preparations; in vivo confirmation limited

unreplicated_claims.claim: Neurogliaform cells operate primarily through volume transmission rather than synaptic contacts
unreplicated_claims.note: While GABA-B receptor activation through volume transmission is well-established in vitro, the relative contribution of volume vs synaptic transmission in vivo remains unclear

unreplicated_claims.claim: Chandelier cells can be excitatory under certain conditions
unreplicated_claims.note: Original finding of depolarized GABA at AIS has been replicated but whether this translates to excitation is still debated
